LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LVOVICH & SZUCSKO, P.C.'S APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING CONTEMPT. PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE CONTEMPT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S AUGUST 13, 2020 ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, THAT DEFENDANT WAS AWARE OF THE ORDER, AND THAT HE HAS WILLFULLY DISOBEYED IT. THE COURT WILL HOLD A HEARING ON MAY 5, 2021 AT 9:30 A.M. TO DETERMINE WHETHER DEFENDANT IS GUILTY OF THE CONTEMPT CHARGED AND, IF SO, THE PENALTY TO BE IMPOSED, WHICH MAY INCLUDE A FINE OF $1,000, PAYABLE TO THE COURT, IMPRISONMENT NOT EXCEEDING FIVE DAYS, AND PAYMENT OF THE REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS INCURRED BY PLAINTIFF IN CONNECTION WITH THE CONTEMPT PROCEEDING, INCLUDING THE INSTANT APPLICATION. PLAINTIFF SHALL PERSONALLY SERVE THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AND SUPPORTING AFFIDAVITS ON DEFENDANT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF LVOVICH & SZUCSKO, P.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. L&S HAS FAILED TO SATISFY ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO $584,002.02 ON EITHER OR BOTH OF ITS BREACH OF CONTRACT AND COMMON COUNTS CLAIMS. BOTH CLAIMS ARE PREMISED ON THE EXISTENCE AND ENFORCEABILITY OF THE APRIL 30, 2012 ATTORNEY-CLIENT FEE AGREEMENT. HOWEVER, SOME OF THE FEES L&S SEEKS ON THIS MOTION WERE FOR SERVICES PURPORTEDLY RENDERED BEFORE THE DATE OF THAT AGREEMENT AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY L&S THAT THE AGREEMENT APPLIES TO THOSE FEES. MOREOVER, THE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FOR A DEED OF TRUST IN FAVOR OF L&S TO BE RECORDED WHICH REQUIRES THAT L&S COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF RULE 3-300 OF THE RULES OF PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT AND L&S HAS NOT SUBMITTED ANY EVIDENCE THAT IT COMPLIED WITH ALL OF THOSE REQUIREMENTS. IN PARTICULAR, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE CONTRACT TERM REGARDING THE DEED OF TRUST WAS FAIR AND REASONABLE TO MR. GALASHOV OR THAT THE EXPLANATION OF THAT TERM WAS DISCLOSED TO HIM IN A MANNER WHICH REASONABLY SHOULD HAVE BEEN UNDERSTOOD BY HIM. FURTHERMORE, WHILE MR. GALASHOV APPARENTLY PUT HIS INITIALS IN THE PORTION OF THE CONTRACT STATING THAT HE WAS GIVEN A REASONABLE OPPORTUNITY TO CONSULT AN INDEPENDENT LAWYER, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS GIVEN SUCH AN OPPORTUNITY AND THE FACT THAT THE CONTRACT IS BOTH DATED AND SIGNED THE SAME DAY SUGGESTS THAT HE MIGHT NOT HAVE BEEN GIVEN SUCH AN OPPORTUNITY. EVEN IF L&S DID SATISFY ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN, MR. GALSAHOV'S DECLARATION CREATES TRIABLE DISPUTES REGARDING L&S' ENTITLEMENT TO FEES FOR SERVICES PURPORTEDLY RENDERED BY IT BEFORE THE DATE OF THE APRIL 30, 2012 CONTRACT AND WHETHER L&S COMPLIED WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF RULE 3-300. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

Order To Show Cause Re Contempt
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Continued To Aug-03-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Terry Szucsko, Esq. of for the Plaintiffs;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Order To Show Cause Re Contempt.

Matter called. Discussions are held.

Mr. Szucsko informs the Court that immediately upon the tentative ruling issuing that Mr. Galashov filed for bankruptcy. Mr. Szucsko requests a 90-day continuance.

The Court continued the matter to August 3, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. The Court orders Mr. Szucsko to prepare an order which is to include the following:

Plaintiff shall file no later than 16 court days before the hearing either an order granting relief or briefing on how the automatic stay does not prevent them from proceeding.
Defendant can respond 9 court days prior to the hearing date.
Defendant shall personally appear.

Mr. Szucsko is prepare a form of order today and submit it to the Court for review and signature.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No., Email:. (302/EPS)

Notice And Application For Order To Show Cause Regarding Contempt
Off Calendar Apr-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF LVOVICH & SZUCSKO, P.C.'s Application For Order To Show Cause Regarding Contempt.

Plaintiff's application for an order to show cause re contempt is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff shows that Defendant failed to comply with the Court's August 13, 2020 order granting Plaintiff's motion to compel discovery responses and request for sanctions, that Defendant was aware of the order, and that he has willfully disobeyed it. The Court will hold a hearing on May 5, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. to determine whether Defendant is guilty of the contempt charged and, if so, the penalty to be imposed, which may include a fine of $1,000, payable to the court, imprisonment not exceeding five days, and payment of the reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred by plaintiff in connection with the contempt proceeding, including the instant application. Plaintiff shall personally serve the order to show cause and supporting affidavits on Defendant.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Continued To Apr-13-21 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514

Mtn To Set Aside Order Re: Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses And Req. For Sanctions From Aug 10, 2020
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Yuri Galashov Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

"THE PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF."

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court did not receive an email contesting the matter and informs the party of the tentative ruling. Defendant in Pro Per informs the Court he was not aware of the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT YURI GALASHOV's Moion To Set Aside Order Re: Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions From Aug 10, 2020.

Yuri Galashov's motion is DENIED. Mr. Galashov's legal argument is incorrectly premised on CCP sec.473(b) (concerning applications for relief from a default or default judgment entered as a result of mistake, surprise, inadvertence or excusable neglect) and CCP sec.473.5 (motion to set aside default). The essential thrust of the motion requests the court to reconsider the discovery order it entered August 14, 2020. The court may not consider an application to reconsider any order unless it complies with the dictates of CCP sec.1008. (CCP sec.1008(e).) Among other requirements, the party making the application must state by affidavit what new or different facts, circumstances or law warrant the court to reconsider the matter and modify, amend, or revoke the prior order. (Id. at subd. (a).) Mr. Galashov's motion does not include the necessary affidavit and lacks any competent admissible evidence to support it.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Temp at pvestal@nvlawllp.com

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Lvovich & Szucsko, P.C.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. L&S has failed to satisfy its summary judgment burden that it is entitled to $584,002.02 on either or both of its breach of contract and common counts claims. Both claims are premised on the existence and enforceability of the April 30, 2012 Attorney-Client Fee Agreement. However, some of the fees L&S seeks on this motion were for services purportedly rendered before the date of that agreement and there is no evidence submitted by L&S that the agreement applies to those fees. Moreover, the agreement provides for a deed of trust in favor of L&S to be recorded which requires that L&S comply with the requirements of Rule 3-300 of the Rules of Professional Conduct and L&S has not submitted any evidence that it complied with all of those requirements. In particular, there is no evidence that the contract term regarding the deed of trust was fair and reasonable to Mr. Galashov or that the explanation of that term was disclosed to him in a manner which reasonably should have been understood by him. Furthermore, while Mr. Galashov apparently put his initials in the portion of the contract stating that he was given a reasonable opportunity to consult an independent lawyer, there is no evidence that he was given such an opportunity and the fact that the contract is both dated and signed the same day suggests that he might not have been given such an opportunity. Even if L&S did satisfy its summary judgment burden, Mr. Galsahov's declaration creates triable disputes regarding L&S' entitlement to fees for services purportedly rendered by it before the date of the April 30, 2012 contract and whether L&S complied with the requirements of Rule 3-300. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
